title: Pucker Up for International Kissing Day!
story: People around the world celebrate International Kissing Day every July 6 by sharing sweet kisses with family and friends! Couples post cute kissing photos on social media, parents give extra goodnight kisses to their kids, and some cities even hold kissing contests. It's a day to show love and affection through one of the simplest gestures humans share across all cultures.
activity_question: What do some cities organize on International Kissing Day?
follow_up_question: What are different ways people show affection in your family or culture?

title: World Zoonoses Day: When Animals and Humans Share More Than Friendship
story: Every July 6, scientists and doctors around the world celebrate World Zoonoses Day to teach people about diseases that can jump from animals to humans. Zoonoses sounds fancy, but it just means illnesses that pets, farm animals, or wild creatures can accidentally pass to people. On this day, veterinarians visit schools to explain how washing hands after petting animals keeps everyone healthy. Animal shelters post tips about safe pet care, and zoos share fun facts about staying safe around different creatures.
activity_choices: Diseases that jump from animals to humans
Animals that can talk to people
activity_answer: Diseases that jump from animals to humans
bonus_fact: Most zoonoses are prevented by simple things like washing hands and keeping pets healthy!

title: Lithuania's Big Birthday Bash
story: Every July 6, Lithuania throws a massive birthday party for their entire country! Lithuania Statehood Day celebrates when this Baltic nation became independent and free. People fill the streets with parades, waving yellow, green, and red flags everywhere you look. Families gather for outdoor concerts and traditional folk dancing. Kids enjoy special treats and games while adults share stories about their proud heritage and freedom.
activity_question: What colors are on Lithuania's flag that people wave during the celebration?
bonus_fact: Lithuania was one of the first countries to break away from the Soviet Union!
